action.reasoning: Observation: inspect_render.py succeeded with correct renderer call model.renderer(model.decoder, triplane, rays_o, rays_d) returning tensor [1,256,256,3] RGB; custom get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth (robust up-vector/eps) + rel_az=(target_az-input_az)%360 works and saved test_render.png. Prior LPIPS=0.6021 used fallback orbit on some objs due to bad renderer call. Analysis: Default orbit (fixed az~0 start) mismatches dataset's large varying relative azimuths (~185° avg) + per-view elev/dist, causing poor alignment and high LPIPS. Correct per-target rays + exact renderer call (per inspected signature) + white-BG input + robust tensor->RGBA PIL (clamp 0-1, CHW for to_pil_image, alpha=255) will yield major LPIPS drop. Zero-shot TripoSR with this is optimal (exact task match, <1B params, no training, fits budget). Decision: Fully rewrite predict.py with complete working code ported from successful inspect (get func, model load/eval, per-obj white-BG input, scene_codes, 11-target loop with exact cameras, renderer call, tensor-to-RGBA conversion+save as view_XX.png, gc/cache, output JSON/dir). Test on full val (~few min), then submit_eval for updated metric+time before considering training.
action.command: python inspect_render.py && python predict.py --data_path /data/val --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_predictions.json --num_objects 20
